worth_it_explanation: A creative that fails to render, runs as a black frame, or blasts the viewer with loud audio reaches the screen before anyone catches it, and on a streaming or Connected TV (CTV) surface the viewer cannot scroll past it the way they can on a web page. This check is a review of the creative-quality controls a publisher already has in SpringServe, so it costs reading time rather than rebuild time, and the finding points to a concrete setting the publisher can confirm or tighten.
intro: Ad quality scanning is the set of controls that inspect a creative before and while it serves so that a malformed Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) response, a broken or unplayable media file, a black or slate frame, or an audio-loudness spike does not reach the viewer. SpringServe sits between demand and the video player as the ad server, which puts it in the position to scan creatives, flag the ones that fail, and decide whether a flagged creative serves anyway. This subcategory looks at whether scanning is in place and whether a flagged creative is actually blocked rather than left to run.

audit_checks.title: Creative scanning for malformed VAST, broken media, and audio loudness is not in place
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without scanning, a creative that returns malformed VAST, points to an unplayable media file, renders as a black or slate frame, or carries a loudness spike reaches the viewer before anyone notices. On a streaming or Connected TV surface the viewer cannot skip past it, so a single bad creative can break the ad break for the whole session and erode trust in the channel.

educate.base:
SpringServe is a video and Connected TV (CTV) ad server, which means it stands between the demand it routes and the video player that renders the ad. That position is what makes creative scanning possible. The ad server receives the Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) response from demand, and before it hands the creative to the player it can inspect the response for the problems that break playback or harm the viewing experience.

Several distinct failures sit under the heading of ad quality. A malformed VAST response fails to parse, so the player cannot read it and the impression is lost. A well-formed response can still point to a media file that the player cannot decode, declares an unsupported MIME (multipurpose internet mail extensions) type, or fails to load, which the IAB Tech Lab VAST specification handles through dedicated error codes for media-file problems. A creative can render as a black frame or a slate where video should be, leaving the viewer staring at nothing during the break. And a creative can carry audio far louder than the surrounding content, which is the problem the broadcast loudness standards were written to address and which reads as a jarring spike to a streaming viewer.

Scanning catches these before they reach the screen rather than after a viewer complains. The audit question is whether SpringServe is configured to inspect creatives across these dimensions, or whether creatives pass through to the player unchecked and the first sign of a problem is a drop in completion rate or a support ticket.

A few signs point to scanning that is not in place:
  • No creative-review or quality-scanning step is configured between demand and the player, so creatives serve as received
  • Malformed VAST, media-file load failures, and black or slate frames surface only through after-the-fact reporting rather than a control that flags them
  • No loudness check exists, so a creative louder than the content around it serves without notice
You may want to confirm that SpringServe inspects creatives for malformed VAST, unplayable media, blank frames, and loudness before they serve. One approach is to review the ad-quality settings on the account and the demand tags, then read error and completion reporting to see whether bad creatives are being caught or only discovered later.

site_type_overlays.publishing: On a streaming or broadcaster property, the ad break interrupts content the viewer chose to watch, so a black frame or a loudness spike during the break stands out far more than a misfire on a scrolling web page. You may want to confirm that scanning covers the full-screen video creatives that run in those breaks because the viewer has no way to scroll past a creative that fails on a living-room screen.
site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a commerce-content video property, the video sits next to product or shopping content meant to move a viewer toward a purchase, so a broken or jarring creative interrupts that intent at the moment it matters. You may want to confirm that scanning runs on the creatives serving against the commercial video inventory, where a failed or loud ad can pull the viewer out of a buying mindset.

learn_more.source_title: A/85: Techniques for Establishing and Maintaining Audio Loudness for Digital Television

educate.learn_more_note: SpringServe's detailed configuration documentation for ad quality scanning is in the partner portal (login required).

investigate.intro: Review whether SpringServe is configured to scan creatives for malformed VAST, media-file failures, black or slate frames, and audio loudness, and read error and completion reporting to see whether bad creatives are being caught before they serve.

springserve_ui.label: SpringServe console
steps: Open the account-level and demand-tag ad-quality settings and note whether creative scanning or creative review is enabled, and which checks it covers.
Record whether the scanning covers malformed VAST, media-file load failures, black or slate frames, and audio loudness, or whether only a subset is configured.
Build or open an error report broken out by VAST error type and demand source, and note the rate of malformed-VAST and media-file errors reaching the player.
Read completion-rate reporting for any drop that points to creatives failing during playback rather than at the request, which suggests a quality problem scanning would catch.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether SpringServe scans creatives for malformed VAST, media-file failures, black or slate frames, and audio loudness, and reads error and completion reporting for evidence of bad creatives reaching the player. A setup where scanning covers each of those dimensions and reporting shows few quality failures slipping through passes. A setup with no scanning configured, or scanning that omits one or more of those dimensions while reporting shows quality failures reaching the player, is a fail recorded with the dimensions left unchecked.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record which creative-quality checks SpringServe runs and what the error and completion reporting shows above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} serves creatives through SpringServe without scanning them for malformed Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) responses, unplayable media files, black or slate frames, and audio loudness. On a streaming or Connected TV (CTV) surface the viewer cannot scroll past a creative that fails, so a bad creative can break the ad break for the whole session and erode trust in the channel. {{voice_recommend}} enabling creative scanning across those dimensions in the account and demand-tag ad-quality settings, then reading error and completion reporting to confirm bad creatives are caught before they reach the player. After the change, recheck the error and completion reports to confirm the quality failures that were slipping through are now flagged.

audit_checks.title: Flagged creatives are left to run rather than blocked
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Scanning that flags a bad creative but still lets it serve catches the problem on paper while the viewer still sees the failure. If the flag does not stop the creative, the scanning produces a record rather than a control, so the broken or loud creative reaches the screen exactly as it would have with no scanning at all.

educate.base:
Scanning a creative and blocking a creative are two separate decisions. A scan inspects the Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) response and the media it points to, and it can flag a creative that fails for a malformed response, an unplayable media file, a black or slate frame, or a loudness problem. Whether that flag stops the creative from serving is a configuration choice the publisher makes, and the two do not always move together.

A scan set to flag but not block produces a record of the problem while the creative continues to reach the viewer. The publisher can see, after the fact, that a creative failed, but the failure already played in the break. On a streaming or Connected TV (CTV) surface that is the whole cost because the viewer watched the black frame or heard the loud audio and the scan changed nothing about that experience. Flagging without blocking turns ad quality scanning into a reporting feature rather than a protection.

The block decision also benefits from a fallback. When SpringServe blocks a creative that failed a scan, the ad server can be set to fill the slot from other demand rather than leave a gap, so blocking a bad creative does not turn into an unfilled break. The audit looks at both halves, whether flagged creatives are actually blocked and whether a fallback fills the slot the blocked creative would have taken.

A few signs point to flagged creatives being left to run:
  • Scanning is enabled and creatives are flagged, but the action on a flagged creative is set to log or allow rather than block
  • Reporting shows creatives flagged for quality problems that still recorded completed impressions, which means they served
  • No fallback or competing-demand path is configured, so blocking is avoided to keep the slot filled
You may want to confirm that a flagged creative is blocked rather than allowed, and that a fallback fills the slot so blocking does not create a gap. One approach is to check the action configured for a failed scan, then read reporting for any flagged creative that still completed an impression.

site_type_overlays.publishing: On a streaming or broadcaster property, a flagged creative that still serves plays full-screen during a break the viewer cannot skip, so flagging without blocking offers no protection where it matters most. You may want to confirm both that flagged creatives are blocked and that competing demand fills the slot because a living-room viewer notices an empty break as readily as a broken ad.
site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a commerce-content video property, a flagged creative that runs anyway interrupts a viewer the publisher is trying to move toward a purchase, so allowing a known-bad creative works against the commercial intent of the inventory. You may want to confirm that flagged creatives are blocked and backfilled from other demand so the slot stays filled with a creative that passed the scan.

educate.learn_more_note: SpringServe's detailed configuration documentation for ad quality scanning is in the partner portal (login required).

investigate.intro: Review the action SpringServe takes on a creative that fails a scan, confirm whether flagged creatives are blocked or allowed, and check whether a fallback fills the slot a blocked creative would have taken.

springserve_ui.label: SpringServe console
steps: Open the ad-quality settings and note the action configured for a failed scan, recording whether a flagged creative is blocked or only logged and allowed to serve.
Record whether a fallback or competing-demand path is configured to fill the slot when a flagged creative is blocked.
Build or open a report broken out by creative and quality flag, and look for any flagged creative that still recorded completed impressions, which means it served despite the flag.
Note any case where blocking is turned off specifically to avoid an unfilled slot, which points to a missing fallback rather than a deliberate quality choice.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the action SpringServe takes on a failed scan and whether a fallback fills the slot a blocked creative would leave. A setup where flagged creatives are blocked and a fallback keeps the slot filled passes. A setup where flagged creatives are logged or allowed and still record completed impressions, or where blocking is disabled because no fallback exists, is a fail recorded with the action configured and the flagged creatives that served.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the action SpringServe takes on a failed scan and whether flagged creatives still served above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} flags creatives that fail a scan in SpringServe but lets them serve rather than blocking them. A scan that flags without blocking produces a record of the problem while the broken or loud creative still reaches the viewer, so the scanning protects nothing on the screen. {{voice_recommend}} setting the action on a failed scan to block the creative, and configuring a fallback so competing demand fills the slot the blocked creative would have taken. After the change, read reporting to confirm that flagged creatives no longer record completed impressions and that the fallback is keeping the slots filled.
